# @agent-os/backend-node-postgres

## Purpose

Node/Postgres production backend interpreter for agentOS backend-protocol
semantics. It provides the second production backend proof for backend-neutral
status alongside the Cloudflare Durable Object backend.

## Public API Status

Backend package. It is the Node/Postgres production interpreter used as the second backend-neutral proof backend.

## Invariant

The backend interprets the same backend-protocol algebra as the Cloudflare DO
backend. Ledger facts live in Postgres, and `due_work` is a mechanical
scheduling and claim buffer, not an audit source.

Concurrent drainers claim due work with Postgres row ownership:
`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, claim tokens, and claim deadlines ensure one due
dispatch retry is acquired at most once across processes. Dispatch retry,
schedule firing, resource reservation, quota grant, telemetry diagnostics, and
replay gates must remain backend-neutral projections of the shared protocol.

The package does not own protocol vocabulary. Dispatch payloads, delivery
receipt settlement, retry policy, resource/quota projection shapes, and replay
snapshot vocabulary stay in `@agent-os/backend-protocol`.

The test harness may start a temporary Docker Postgres instance when
`AGENTOS_NODE_POSTGRES_DATABASE_URL` is not set. That harness is not public API.

## Minimal Usage

Create a backend with an explicit Postgres database URL and binding ref, then
initialize its schema before use.

```ts
import { NodePostgresBackend } from "@agent-os/backend-node-postgres";
import { bindingMaterialRef } from "@agent-os/kernel/material-ref";

const backend = new NodePostgresBackend({
  databaseUrl: process.env.AGENTOS_NODE_POSTGRES_DATABASE_URL!,
  bindingRef: bindingMaterialRef({
    provider: "node",
    bindingKind: "postgres",
    ref: "agent",
  }),
});

await backend.initialize();
```

## Verification

```sh
cd packages/backends/node-postgres
bun run test:runtime
```
